The key question is: can the 6x6 box cross the purple bar? So far, both the 6x3 and 6x6 boxes have been blocked when trying to cross the purple divider.
Let me check the current position of the 6x6 box and see if there's any way to get it across.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the current position of the 6x6 box
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] >= 20:
        print(f"Green box: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Also check the 6x3 box
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N' and 10 < n['pixels'] < 20:
        print(f"Small green box: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check animation details to see how many LEFTs actually moved the box
if last_animation and len(last_animation) > 0:
    for i, entry in enumerate(last_animation):
        print(f"Action {i}: {entry.action}, frames={len(entry.frames)}, changed={entry.frames[-1] is not None if entry.frames else None}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now, the key question remains: can boxes cross the purple divider? So far, the answer seems to be no.

- Bottom-right: 2 charcoal boxes (5x5 and 5x8), 4 green boxes

The 2 green boxes in bottom-right have been placed in the 2 charcoal boxes in bottom-right. The remaining 2 green boxes (6x3 and 6x6) need to be placed in the charcoal boxes in top-left and bottom-left. But they can't cross the purple divider.
